corpus. The application was made
without notice to the prosecution.
Separate petitions were filed for
Meyer, Haywood and Pettibone.
They allege arrest as a result of
on the part of Governor
Gooding, Prosecution Attorney Van
Duyn, Caldwell and Hawley, chief
counsel for the prosecution. The
prisoners are not charged with con-
spiracy to murder or as accessories
but with directly committing the
act, The applicants were taken in
Colorado late at night, with no OPP.
portunity to consult lawyers, de-
MED every right guaranteed by the
constitutions of the United States,
Idaho and Colorado. They are now
held in the penitentiary in close
confinement and denied permission
to consult each other or attorneys.
The matter was taken under adj
visement and an opinion will be
rendered at 5 o'clock this after
noon.
